Component.Name: Mission Control State Machine Component
Component.Purpose: Orchestrates the top-level mission behavior of the robot across IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, and COMPLETED phases. It runs once per control-loop iteration, reads system status (initialization, sensor validity, exploration status, timeouts, planning results), advances an internal finite-state machine, and issues high-level commands to subordinate modules: - Requests frontier targets from the Frontier Generation component during exploration. - Requests path computations from the Path Planning component (exploration targets, return-to-start, second-run). - Requests path execution from the Motion Control component. - Interfaces with the Exploration-Status and Time-Limit logic to decide when exploration is complete or timed out. - Manages second-run success/failure status and provides it for external reporting. It is strictly reactive: all work triggered by control-loop ticks and asynchronous events delivered via queues/flags evaluated in the loop.

Signal.Name: MC_FrontierRequest
Signal.Description: Request structure sent from Mission Control to Frontier Generation during EXPLORE. Contains: - requester_id = CMP--1 - current_cell (row, col) from pose estimate - optional max_frontiers (uint16) limiting number of returned cells (can be 0 for all) - exploration_mode flag (bool) distinguishing exploration vs planning queries Semantics: triggers recomputation/lookup of frontier set; synchronous call within same control-loop iteration.

Signal.Name: MC_PathPlanRequest
Signal.Description: Request sent to Path Planning component. Struct fields: - type: {EXPLORE_TO_FRONTIER, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN_TO_GOAL} - start_cell: (row, col) - goal_spec: * for EXPLORE_TO_FRONTIER: target frontier cell (row, col) * for RETURN_TO_START: start_cell coordinate from map module * for SECOND_RUN_TO_GOAL: goal_region description (set of goal cells) - cost_model_profile: * EXPLORATION_PROFILE: lower speed, cost tuned to balance distance and information gain * SECOND_RUN_PROFILE: cost tuned to approximate travel time and penalize turns - time_constraint_ms: remaining allowed exploration time (for EXPLORE) or 0 if no constraint Request is synchronous within control-loop: caller blocks until Path Plan response is produced or failure is signaled in the same tick.

Signal.Name: MC_PathExecutionCommand
Signal.Description: High-level command to Motion Control component indicating what path to execute: - path_handle or path_sequence (ordered list of (row, col, heading) waypoints) - execution_mode: {EXPLORE_MODE, RETURN_MODE, SECOND_RUN_MODE} - allow_replanning: bool - emergency_stop_enabled: bool - target_reached_tolerance: max deviation (cell and heading) allowed to consider waypoint reached. Mission Control issues this when a new path is available or needs to be (re)started.

Signal.Name: MC_ReturnToStartInvoke
Signal.Description: Logical signal/event from Mission Control to Return-to-Start service (in Path Planning / Motion Control stack) indicating exploration has ended. Contains: - trigger_reason: {MAP_COMPLETE, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT} - current_cell - start_cell (from grid map) Used to start RETURN_TO_START computation and execution.

Signal.Name: MC_SecondRunInvoke
Signal.Description: Signal to Second-Run services to start fastest-path planning and execution. Contains: - confirmation that current_cell == start_cell - selected_goal_region (2x2 FREE block) - second_run_profile configuration (max speed, acceleration limits).

Signal.Name: FG_FrontierSet
Signal.Description: Response from Frontier Generation: - frontier_cells: array of (row, col) - timestamp - valid: bool - updated_since_last: bool If empty and map not complete, exploration module may signal inability to proceed; otherwise selection heuristic chooses a target.

Signal.Name: PP_PathPlanResponse
Signal.Description: Response from Path Planner: - status: {SUCCESS, NO_PATH} - path: list of waypoints ((row, col, heading)) if SUCCESS, else empty - estimated_travel_time_ms for this path, computed from traversal costs and nominal cell traversal time - invalidation_subscription_token: opaque handle used to subscribe for map change invalidation events.

Signal.Name: MC_ExplorationStatusEvents
Signal.Description: Events pulled from system event queue created by Exploration Status component: - type: {EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT} - timestamp - map_complete_flag (for COMPLETED) Mission Control uses these to trigger transition from EXPLORE to RETURN_TO_START.

Signal.Name: INIT_COMPLETED_Indication
Signal.Description: Flag or event from Initialization module indicating all initialization done. Mission Control reads it each loop: - representation: volatile bool or event queue record - once consumed, remains latched in internal MC state to avoid missing transient events.

Signal.Name: SensorValidityStatus
Signal.Description: From Sensor Abstraction: - has_valid_ultrasonic: bool - has_valid_ir: bool - last_ultrasonic.distance_cm, timestamp - last_ir.left_flag, right_flag, timestamp Used for Exploration Start Condition decision and to ensure moves are not initiated without at least one valid full sensor set.

Signal.Name: PathExecutionStatus
MC_MapCompletenessQuery
Signal.Description: Query to Grid Map / Map Completeness logic to obtain: - is_complete: bool - unknown_cell_count - unknown_wall_count Used to determine if exploration is done (in addition to Exploration Status events).

Signal.Name: MC_TimeRemainingQuery
Signal.Description: Query to Time-Aware Planning / Exploration Status: - elapsed_time_ms since exploration start - remaining_time_ms before 5 min limit Mission Control uses remaining_time_ms when forming MC_PathPlanRequest for exploration targets to respect time constraints.

Signal.Name: MapCompletenessIndication
Signal.Description: Boolean or struct from map module: - is_complete: bool - last_complete_change_timestamp This indication is also used by Exploration-Status module; MC double-checks state as safeguard.

Signal.Name: RemainingExplorationTimeInfo
Signal.Description: From Time-Aware Planning: - elapsed_time_ms - remaining_time_ms - global_limit_ms (default 300000)

Signal.Name: MC_SecondRunStatusOutput
Signal.Description: Output provided to external reporting and higher-level modules at COMPLETED state: - status: {NOT_RUN, SUCCESS, FAILURE, ABORTED} - completion_timestamp It remains latched and readable indefinitely after mission completion.

Signal.Name: ExternalMissionControlCommands
Signal.Description: Optional external control commands (e.g., to reset mission, start new mission) if supported: - command_type: {RESET_MISSION, START_SECOND_RUN_ONLY, NONE} - parameters Not directly covered by LLRs but reserved for extension; current design may ignore.

Component.Execution: Executed once per iteration of the periodic control loop scheduled by CMP--8 (Scheduling/Control Loop). Pre-conditions per cycle: - Sensor Abstraction layer has delivered latest sensor readings to shared state. - Grid map and pose modules have updated their internal states for current cycle. - Event queue from Exploration-Status has been updated. Mission Control's finite state machine processes: 1. Poll INIT_COMPLETED indication and sensor validity. 2. Process any pending exploration events (EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT). 3. Based on current FSM state and inputs (time remaining, map completeness, path execution status), compute deterministic next state. 4. Issue at most one new high-level command per cycle (e.g., plan request, path execution start/stop), ensuring side-effect-free read operations for map and sensors.
Component.Timing: - Executes at the control-loop frequency T defined by CMP--8 (Periodic Control Loop Scheduling). - Control-loop frequency equals sensor sampling rate in [10, 50] Hz (default 20 Hz). - All MC operations are deterministic and must complete well within one cycle budget (e.g., < 20% of T at default rate) assuming path planning may execute within the same thread; if planning is heavy, planning can be split over multiple cycles but MC must treat partial-planning state explicitly. - Exploration timeout limit: 5 minutes (300000 ms) from EXPLORE entry time. Remaining time is computed each cycle. - MC must not issue more than one re-planning request per cycle per path type (to avoid planner overload).
Component.Invariants: - FSM state is always one of {IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, COMPLETED}; no other state values allowed. - State transitions are deterministic: for a given current state and input set (INIT_COMPLETED flag, events, time, completion flags), at most one outgoing transition is taken. - EXPLORE may only be entered once per mission; RETURN_TO_START may only be entered after EXPLORE; SECOND_RUN may only be entered after RETURN_TO_START; COMPLETED may only be entered after SECOND_RUN or after an unrecoverable failure. - Mission Control shall never issue motion commands directly; it only commands Motion Control via high-level path execution interfaces, preserving separation of concerns. - Mission Control shall not transition from IDLE to EXPLORE unless: * INIT_COMPLETED == true, and * SensorValidityStatus indicates at least one valid ultrasonic and IR measurement (left and right available). - SECOND_RUN status output is only written while in SECOND_RUN or on transition to COMPLETED; once in COMPLETED it becomes read-only.
Component.Failures: - Planning Failure in RETURN_TO_START: * Condition: Path Planner responds NO_PATH to MC_PathPlanRequest with type RETURN_TO_START. * Behavior: Mission Control sets an internal "return_to_start_failed" flag, skips SECOND_RUN, and transitions to COMPLETED with second-run status FAILURE. Robot remains stopped (no motion commands issued). - Planning Failure in SECOND_RUN: * Condition: Path Planner responds NO_PATH for SECOND_RUN_TO_GOAL. * Behavior: Mission Control sets second run status FAILURE, transitions to COMPLETED, and keeps robot stopped. - Exploration No-Frontier Condition: * Condition: Frontier set is empty while map completeness is false and remaining_time_ms > 0. * Behavior: Mission Control treats this as exploration-completed due to lack of reachable frontiers, publishes EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event via Exploration-Status interface or directly triggers RETURN_TO_START. - Emergency Stop During Any Path Execution: * Condition: Motion Control reports PathExecutionStatus.status == COLLISION_EMERGENCY_STOP. * Behavior: Mission Control suspends current state transition: - In EXPLORE: attempt replanning to nearest safe cell or new frontier; if repeated failures or no path, treat as exploration failure and go to RETURN_TO_START or COMPLETED depending on circumstances. - In RETURN_TO_START: attempt replanning; if repeated failures or NO_PATH, mark mission as failed and go to COMPLETED. - In SECOND_RUN: immediately mark second-run FAILURE and transition to COMPLETED. - Timeout While Planning: * Condition: Global mapping time limit reached while planner still computing exploration path. * Behavior: Exploration-Status publishes EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event. On next cycle, MC disregards pending exploration plan and transitions from EXPLORE to RETURN_TO_START.

Component.Name: Frontier Generation and Exploration Target Selection Component
Component.Purpose: Implements frontier-based exploration. Responsibilities: - Maintains and recomputes the set of frontier cells: cells in UNKNOWN state that are 4-neighbors of at least one FREE cell. - Provides API to obtain current frontier set on demand from Mission Control or planner. - Implements exploration target selection heuristic that: * Uses traversal cost model consistent with Path Planner for travel-time estimate. * Adds heuristic term for expected information gain (i.e., number of newly observable UNKNOWN cells from that frontier). * Filters out frontiers whose estimated reach-and-explore time exceeds remaining exploration time. - Exposes chosen target as a cell coordinate for path planning requests.

Signal.Name: FG_TargetSelectionMapQueries
MapClassificationChangeEvent
Signal.Description: Event from grid map access interface: - changed_cells: list of (row, col, old_state, new_state) - changed_walls: list of (wall_id, old_state, new_state) Used to trigger recomputation of frontier set when a cell changes from UNKNOWN to FREE/BLOCKED or wall segments change from UNKNOWN.

Signal.Name: FG_FrontierSet
Signal.Description: See CMP--1.Receive.FG_FrontierSet. This component computes frontier set and returns it to Mission Control.

Signal.Name: MC_FrontierRequest
Signal.Description: See CMP--1.Transmit.MC_FrontierRequest. Used to trigger frontier computation or to access cached frontiers.

Signal.Name: RemainingExplorationTimeInfo
Signal.Description: Provided by Time-Aware Planning or Mission Control: - remaining_time_ms - nominal_cell_traversal_ms Used to filter candidate targets that cannot be explored within remaining time.

Signal.Name: FG_TargetCostEstimationRequest
Signal.Description: For each candidate frontier, FG may call into Path Planner for: - estimated shortest path cost (in arbitrary cost units or time) from current cell to frontier under exploration cost profile. This can be implemented using: - request_estimated_cost_only(start, goal) (no full path required) or - reuse of path planning algorithm but discarding path and keeping cost.

Signal.Name: PP_TargetCostEstimationResponse
Signal.Description: Response from Path Planner to cost estimation: - success: bool - estimated_cost (float) - estimated_travel_time_ms If success==false or infinite cost, frontier is treated as unreachable.

Component.Execution: - Runs in two main modes: 1) Event-driven frontier update: * On each MapClassificationChangeEvent where old_state == UNKNOWN and new_state in {FREE, BLOCKED} for cell or wall changes, recompute frontier set incrementally: - For changed cell (r,c): evaluate its 4-neighbors and itself to see whether they now meet frontier definition. - For wall changes, reevaluate adjacency relationships as needed. * Complexity O(k) for number of local neighbors. 2) On-demand frontier query and target selection: * On MC_FrontierRequest in EXPLORE, ensure frontier set is current (may recalc if stale), then return full set or subset according to request. * When asked to select a best target (either within this component or by exposing scoring function to MC), for each candidate frontier: - Use get_neighbors to approximate expected number of newly observable UNKNOWN cells (information gain). - Request path cost estimate from Path Planner or compute Manhattan-like heuristic consistent with cost model. - Convert path cost to estimated travel time using nominal_cell_traversal_ms and cost parameters. - Combine metrics: H(frontier) = alpha * travel_time + beta * (-expected_new_cells), with tunable alpha,beta, and pick minimum H subject to constraint travel_time <= remaining_time_ms. - Executed within the control-loop thread when invoked so all updates are single-threaded with respect to map access.
Component.Timing: - No hard real-time constraints, but operations must complete within the control loop period. - Frontier incremental updates should run in O(1) per changed cell/wall, ensuring scalability. - Target selection complexity per cycle: O(F) where F is current frontier count; with 4x8 maze, F is bounded by 32. - In practice, frontier selection must finish within ~25% of control loop period at default 20 Hz.
Component.Invariants: - Maintains data structure frontier_set where each (row, col) satisfies: * cell_state(row, col) == UNKNOWN * exists a neighbor (row', col') in 4-neighborhood such that cell_state(row', col') == FREE and wall_state between them != PRESENT. - No duplicates in frontier_set. - After any map cell changes from UNKNOWN to non-UNKNOWN, that cell is removed from frontier_set if present. - frontier_set fully reflects grid map contents when there are no pending classification changes. - Heuristic scores for targets are recomputed when either remaining_time_ms or traversal cost parameters change.
Component.Failures: - No Candidate Frontiers: * Condition: frontier_set is empty and map is not complete. * Behavior: Component signals empty frontier set to MC. MC may treat as implicit exploration completion or failure. - Target Exceeds Remaining Time: * Condition: For all candidates, estimated_travel_time_ms > remaining_time_ms. * Behavior: Component returns no valid target; MC then triggers exploration timeout or map-complete check. - Planner Cost Estimation Failure: * Condition: Path Planner signals failure for cost estimation or path not found. * Behavior: Mark that frontier as unreachable and skip it in heuristic; if all skipped, treat as no-candidate case.

Component.Name: Grid Map and Classification Component
Component.Purpose: Provides central representation of the 4x8 maze grid including: - Cell state enumeration {UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED}. - Inter-cell wall state enumeration {UNKNOWN, PRESENT, ABSENT}. - Indexing of cells and inter-cell walls based on a consistent (row, col) coordinate system and wall boundary indexing. Responsibilities: - Initialize all cells/walls to UNKNOWN at mission start. - Initialize designated start cell as FREE and record its coordinate. - Maintain immutable classification semantics: cells and walls change only from UNKNOWN to specific definitive states and never revert or toggle between definitive states. - Update cell and wall classifications based on Mapping component requests (sensor-derived) and reliability constraints (consistency checks). - Provide read and write interface to other modules (planning, frontier generation, etc.). - Maintain and compute map completeness status. - Provide classification-change events to registered listeners (e.g., Frontier Generation, Replanning).

Signal.Name: MapUpdateRequests
Signal.Description: From Mapping Component: requests to update cell or wall states: - type: {SET_CELL_STATE, SET_WALL_STATE} - cell: (row, col) when applicable - wall: wall_id describing boundary between (r1,c1) and (r2,c2) - requested_state: new classification Map enforces static assumptions and consistency checks before applying.

Signal.Name: MapUpdateAcknowledge
Signal.Description: Map module may respond with success/failure: - success: bool - actual_new_state: enumeration - reason: {OK, INVALID_TRANSITION, CONSISTENCY_REJECTED}

Signal.Name: MapClassificationChangeEvent
Signal.Description: See CMP--2.Receive.MapClassificationChangeEvent. Generated when cell or wall classification changes from UNKNOWN to definitive state.

Signal.Name: FG_TargetSelectionMapQueries
Signal.Description: See CMP--2.Transmit.FG_TargetSelectionMapQueries. Map responds synchronously to these queries.

Signal.Name: PlannerMapQueries
Signal.Description: From Planner: queries to get cell and wall states and neighbors for constructing graph: - get_cell_state - get_wall_state - get_reachable_neighbors

Signal.Name: PlannerMapAccess
Signal.Description: Equivalent to PlannerMapQueries; Map implements these APIs.

Signal.Name: MapCompletenessIndication
Signal.Description: See CMP--1.Receive.MapCompletenessIndication. Map module provides a boolean is_complete and maintains it up-to-date.

Signal.Name: StartCellInformation
Signal.Description: Start cell coordinate data: - start_cell_row - start_cell_col - start_heading (optional) for orientation reference. Provided on request for Return-to-Start and Second Run logic.

Signal.Name: MC_MapCompletenessQuery
Signal.Description: Query for completeness; triggers instantaneous evaluation (no heavy computation).

Component.Execution: - Initialization phase: * On system startup before exploration: - Initialize a 4x8 array of cells; set each cell.state = UNKNOWN. - For each possible wall boundary between horizontally and vertically adjacent cells (using Inter-Cell Wall Indexing), create a wall segment record and set wall.state = UNKNOWN. - Apply start cell init: designate cell (row0,col0) as start (e.g., (0,0)), set state = FREE. - Mark map_complete flag as false. - Runtime operations: * On each control-loop iteration (single-threaded): - Process queued MapUpdateRequests from Mapping component: - For cells: if current_state == UNKNOWN and new_state in {FREE, BLOCKED}, commit and emit classification-change event; ignore if transition invalid (e.g., FREE->BLOCKED). - For walls: if current_state == UNKNOWN and new_state in {PRESENT, ABSENT}, commit and emit event; ignore invalid transitions. - Update completeness: - On any change from UNKNOWN to definitive, maintain a count of UNKNOWN cells/walls; if both counts reach zero, set map_complete = true, else if any UNKNOWN present, map_complete = false. - Support read queries for map state from Planner, Frontier Generator, Pose, etc. - All operations must be deterministic and performed within the control-loop cycle.
Component.Timing: - Map update and query operations are O(1) per cell or wall using array indices and precomputed adjacency lists. - Completeness updates are O(1) using counters (no need to scan entire map). - All operations are invoked from within the periodic control loop (CMP--8) ensuring no multi-threaded access and no additional synchronization.
Component.Invariants: - For each cell c: * c.state in {UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED}. * State transitions allowed only UNKNOWN -> FREE or UNKNOWN -> BLOCKED. * Once in FREE or BLOCKED, never transitions to any other value for the current mission. - For each wall segment w: * w.state in {UNKNOWN, PRESENT, ABSENT}. * State transitions allowed only UNKNOWN -> PRESENT or UNKNOWN -> ABSENT. * Once in PRESENT or ABSENT, never transitions to other value for the mission. - Map completeness flag: * map_complete == true iff all cells and walls have non-UNKNOWN state. - Coordinate convention: * 0 <= row < 4, 0 <= col < 8. * Start cell is fixed coordinate (e.g., row 0, col 0) across modules.
Component.Failures: - Invalid Update Request: * Condition: attempt to change FREE -> BLOCKED or PRESENT -> ABSENT, etc. * Behavior: reject update, keep previous state, optionally log or count rejection. Does not affect completeness counters. - Out-of-Range Access: * Condition: query or update request with row/col or wall indices outside defined range. * Behavior: return error code to caller, ignore update; no state change. - Consistency Violations from Reliability Checks: * Condition: Mapping or Reliability modules disallow update due to inconsistent sensor evidence. * Behavior: Map returns INVALID_TRANSITION or CONSISTENCY_REJECTED; caller responsible for handling.

Component.Name: Exploration Status and Time Limit Component
Component.Purpose: Tracks exploration progress and global time limits. Responsibilities: - Start and track mission time from EXPLORE state entry. - Maintain configurable global exploration time limit (default 5 minutes). - Compute elapsed and remaining exploration time. - Observe map completeness (via CMP--3) and global time to decide when exploration is complete versus timed-out. - Publish EXPLORATION_COMPLETED and EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT events through a system event queue accessible to Mission Control and Return-to-Start logic.

Signal.Name: ExplorationStatusEvents
RemainingTimeInfo
Signal.Description: Synchronous data provided to MC: - elapsed_time_ms since EXPLORE entry - remaining_time_ms until global_limit_ms - global_limit_ms (default 300000)

Signal.Name: MC_ExplorationPhaseNotification
Signal.Description: Notification from MC when entering or leaving EXPLORE: - on_enter_explore(timestamp_ms) - on_exit_explore(timestamp_ms, reason). Used to start/stop counters and lock-in events.

Signal.Name: MapCompletenessPoll
Signal.Description: Poll of map completeness: - is_complete: bool - unknown_cell_count - unknown_wall_count.

Signal.Name: MapCompletenessIndication
Signal.Description: As defined in CMP--3.Transmit.MapCompletenessIndication; used to detect completion condition.

Signal.Name: TimeParameterQueries
Signal.Description: Queries to get nominal cell traversal duration used for time-aware planning: - nominal_cell_traversal_ms (configurable)

Signal.Name: NominalCellTraversalConfiguration
Signal.Description: Configuration data: - nominal_cell_traversal_ms - time_aware_planning_enabled: bool Received from configuration storage at startup.

Component.Execution: - On startup: * Load global_limit_ms (default 300000 ms). * Load nominal_cell_traversal_ms parameter. - On receiving MC_ExplorationPhaseNotification.on_enter_explore: * Record exploration_start_time_ms from scheduling time base. * Reset flags exploration_completed and exploration_timeout to false. - On each control-loop iteration while in EXPLORE: * Compute elapsed_time_ms = current_time_ms - exploration_start_time_ms. * Compute remaining_time_ms = max(global_limit_ms - elapsed_time_ms, 0). * Poll map completeness: if map_complete == true and exploration_completed == false: - Set exploration_completed = true. - Enqueue EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event. * If elapsed_time_ms >= global_limit_ms and exploration_timeout == false: - Set exploration_timeout = true. - Enqueue EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event. - After leaving EXPLORE, no new events are generated.
Component.Timing: - Runs once per control-loop iteration (within CMP--8 sequence) with O(1) operations. - Timer accuracy must be sufficient so that exploration duration is accurate within control-loop jitter tolerance; scheduling module provides current_time_ms or delta times.
Component.Invariants: - At most one EXPLORATION_COMPLETED and one EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event per mission. - EXPLORATION_COMPLETED may be generated only when map_complete == true and while in EXPLORE phase. - EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT may be generated only when elapsed_time_ms >= global_limit_ms and while in EXPLORE phase. - After EXPLORE has been exited, exploration start, elapsed, and remaining time remain constant and read-only.
Component.Failures: - Time Base Failure: * Condition: Scheduling component does not advance current_time_ms. * Behavior: elapsed_time_ms remains constant; thus timeout never occurs; map completion alone drives exploration completion. - Map Completeness Misreport: * Condition: due to bug, map_complete toggles incorrectly. * Behavior: component may emit EXPLORATION_COMPLETED prematurely; reliance on Map invariants should prevent this.

Component.Name: Path Planning and Goal Region Component
Component.Purpose: Implements cell-based path planning and goal-region selection: - Provides Dijkstra or A* algorithm operating on grid map where: * Nodes: FREE cells. * Edges: 4-connected adjacency not separated by PRESENT walls. - Supports path optimality criterion using traversal cost model that differentiates straight moves and turns. - Handles goal region semantics where target can be any of several cells (2x2 FREE region). - Supports replanning when map updates invalidate current paths. - Reports planning failures when no valid path exists. - Provides specialized services: * Return-to-start path computation. * Fastest path computation for second run by configuring cost parameters to approximate time.

Signal.Name: PlannerMapAccess
Signal.Description: Path Planner queries map: - get_cell_state(row, col) - get_wall_state(boundary) - iterate over all cells to find FREE nodes for goal-region selection.

Signal.Name: MapClassificationChangeEvent
Signal.Description: Subscribed events from grid map used for: - Invalidation of current planned paths when a FREE cell or ABSENT wall becomes BLOCKED or PRESENT (although such transitions are disallowed by map invariants, they may occur only when previously UNKNOWN is clarified to BLOCKED/PRESENT, causing path invalidation).

Signal.Name: PP_PathPlanResponse
Signal.Description: See CMP--1.Receive.PP_PathPlanResponse. Contains path, status, estimated travel time, invalidation token.

Signal.Name: MC_PathPlanRequest
Signal.Description: See CMP--1.Transmit.MC_PathPlanRequest. Path Planner executes algorithm accordingly.

Signal.Name: PP_ElapsedTimeUsage
Signal.Description: When performing time-aware planning for exploration, Path Planner obtains: - remaining_time_ms - nominal_cell_traversal_ms Possibly used to discard paths not feasible in given time.

Signal.Name: RemainingTimeInfo
Signal.Description: Same as in CMP--4.Transmit.RemainingTimeInfo.

Signal.Name: GoalRegionSelectionQuery
Signal.Description: For second-run: - Request to identify candidate 2x2 FREE blocks and select the one minimizing path cost from start cell.

Signal.Name: GoalRegionSelectionResult
Signal.Description: - selected_region: set of 2x2 cell coordinates or empty if none. - candidate_regions: list of all 2x2 reachable eigen-regions for debugging or analysis.

Component.Execution: - Core planning: * Represent each FREE cell as node; edges exist between orthogonally adjacent FREE cells where wall state between them is not PRESENT. * Edge cost computed as: - base_cost (e.g., 1.0 for movement between adjacent cells) multiplied by factor depending on movement direction and whether a turn is needed from previous move. - For time-optimal second run, edge cost approximates travel time: cost = distance / speed + turning_penalty. * Implement A* with heuristic: - For general planning: Manhattan distance scaled by straight-travel cost. - For time-aware: Manhattan distance scaled by nominal_cell_traversal_ms. - Endpoints: * For simple start-goal cell pairs: - Run A*/Dijkstra from start; stop when reaching goal, returning minimal cost path. * For goal region (2x2): - Run multi-goal search or run search until all goal cells visited; choose minimal cost ending among them. - Replanning: * Maintain current active path as list of cells. * When MapClassificationChangeEvent indicates new BLOCKED cell or PRESENT wall intersecting current path, call replanning if those nodes/edges appear in path. - Return-to-start: * Called via dedicated MC_PathPlanRequest of type RETURN_TO_START; set goal to start cell coordinate and use normal cost model. - Fastest path computation for second run: * Called after exploration complete and start cell reached; configure cost model parameters to approximate physical time (higher penalty for turns; speeds according to second-run profile).
Component.Timing: - Maze is 4x8 cells (32 nodes); planning algorithm complexity is small (O(V log V + E)). - Must complete within a single control-loop iteration at 20 Hz; if not, design fallback to splitting planning over multiple cycles while MC waits for completion with timeouts.
Component.Invariants: - Planner only considers cells with state FREE as traversable nodes. - Planner never generates edges through PRESENT walls. - All returned paths begin at start cell given in request and end at a valid goal cell or in a valid goal region. - When no path exists under current map, response status = NO_PATH.
Component.Failures: - No Path Exists: * Behavior: send PP_PathPlanResponse.status = NO_PATH; MC must handle as failure to proceed. - Inconsistent Map: * If map API returns contradictions (e.g., neighbor but wall PRESENT), planner accepts Map behavior as authoritative; edges through PRESENT walls are not added.

Component.Name: Mapping, Wall Detection, and Collision Avoidance Component
Component.Purpose: Integrates sensor data with pose estimation to: - Detect front walls using ultrasonic measurements and the ultrasonic uncertainty model and robot geometry. - Detect side walls from infrared sensors based on alignment and sensor geometry. - Apply wall proximity thresholds and hysteresis to avoid classification flip-flopping. - Update grid map cell and wall states from sensor-derived detections, including marking traversed cells as FREE and inferring BLOCKED cells from PRESENT walls. - Execute wall avoidance maneuvers when imminently intersecting a wall within one cell length, coordinating with Motion Control. - Enforce minimum clearance margin M and manage emergency stop on imminent collision using filtered sensor data. - Perform consistency checks on sensor data before altering established map classifications.

Signal.Name: RawSensorDataRequests
Signal.Description: Mapping uses Sensor Abstraction Layer: - get_filtered_ultrasonic(): returns (distance_cm, timestamp, valid) - get_filtered_ir(): returns (left_flag, right_flag, timestamp, valid) - get_sampling_rate_hz()

Signal.Name: FilteredSensorData
Signal.Description: Provided once per control loop from Sensor Abstraction: - ultrasonic: (distance_cm, timestamp, valid_flag) - ir: (left_flag, right_flag, timestamp, valid_flag) - invalid flags already handled by replacing with safe defaults or ignoring.

Signal.Name: PoseEstimateQueries
Signal.Description: Mapping queries Pose: - get_current_cell(): (row, col) - get_heading(): discrete heading (N,E,S,W) - get_alignment_error(): orientation deviation from nearest axis in degrees - get_cell_crossing_events(): notifications when robot enters new cell.

Signal.Name: CellCenterEntryNotification
Signal.Description: From Pose: indicates crossing from one cell center to adjacent cell center: - from_cell, to_cell, transition_direction. Used to mark traversed cells as FREE, update wall inference for entry face.

Signal.Name: MapUpdateRequests
Signal.Description: See CMP--3.Transmit.MapUpdateRequests: - Set wall segment state PRESENT/ABSENT based on wall detections. - Set cell states FREE when traversed; set BLOCKED for neighbors behind PRESENT walls.

Signal.Name: MapUpdateAcknowledge
Signal.Description: See CMP--3.Receive.MapUpdateAcknowledge, so Mapping knows whether update applied.

Signal.Name: AvoidanceAndEmergencyCommands
CurrentMotionProfile
Signal.Description: From Motion Control: - current_speeds (left,right) - current_translational_speed_cm_s - current_planned_trajectory_segment (for look-ahead collision prediction).

Component.Execution: - On each control-loop iteration: 1) Retrieve filtered ultrasonic and IR sensor data via Sensor Abstraction. 2) Use Pose component to determine: - current cell and heading, - whether robot is near/at cell center or in transition. 3) Front wall detection: - Interpret ultrasonic distance d with uncertainty interval [d-12cm, d+12cm]. - Compute expected robot-center-to-wall distance for current cell/pose using cell size=17.2cm and robot radius=5.5cm plus wall thickness 1.6cm. - If intervals intersect, classify front wall PRESENT at entry boundary between current cell and next cell in heading direction. - Apply numeric thresholds and hysteresis to avoid classification toggling: * Maintain low/high thresholds for wall vs free, update classification only if sequence of measurements crosses thresholds consistently. 4) Side wall detection: - If |alignment_error| <= configured tolerance (max 10 degrees), interpret left/right IR flags: * When active_flag == true, treat as side wall, using nominal 4cm distance plus 25-degree mounting angle and robot geometry to map to nearest cell boundary on left/right side. 5) Cell and wall updates: - For each wall detection: * Determine map boundary index; issue MapUpdateRequests for wall segments with state PRESENT or ABSENT. - For cell traversal: * On CellCenterEntryNotification (robot successfully moves into new cell without collision): - Mark entered cell as FREE (if UNKNOWN). - Mark entry face wall as ABSENT if no blocking wall observed and robot passed through. - Blocked cell inference: * Whenever wall between current cell and neighbor is PRESENT, mark neighbor cell as BLOCKED. 6) Consistency checks and reliability: - When new observation conflicts with existing map classification (e.g., sensor indicates wall where map has ABSENT or FREE): * Maintain counter of consecutive contradictory readings per cell/wall. * Only change classification after N consecutive contradictions (N configurable, default 3, 2 <= N <=10). 7) Collision detection and avoidance: - Calculate predicted nearest wall distance along current trajectory using map and sensor measurements. - Ensure minimum clearance margin M between robot radius and walls, accounting for ultrasonic uncertainty ±12cm. - If predicted collision within one cell length: * Compute avoidance maneuver respecting robot footprint and clearance. * Issue avoidance_maneuver_request to Motion Control. - If filtered sensor data indicates obstacle within emergency distance shorter than stopping distance at current speed: * Issue emergency_stop_request (set both motor speeds to 0). * Motion Control must refrain from resuming until a new safe trajectory set.
Component.Timing: - All computations performed once per control-loop iteration. - Each phase (wall detection, updates, consistency checks) is O(1) with fixed maze size. - Collision checks must use current motor speeds and sampling rate (10-50Hz) to ensure that computed stopping distance is less than (measured_distance - emergency_threshold - uncertainty_margin).
Component.Invariants: - Wall and cell updates respect static-map invariants enforced by CMP--3 (UNKNOWN->FREE/BLOCKED, UNKNOWN->PRESENT/ABSENT only). - Minimum clearance margin M is strictly enforced in collision prediction (no planned or executed ideal trajectory violates M). - Emergency stop has highest priority: once triggered, no movement resumes until Motion Control acknowledges new safe trajectory setup. - Valid sensor checks ensure invalid/out-of-range readings are never directly used for classification or control decisions.
Component.Failures: - Spurious Obstacle Detection: * Effect: Could invoke unnecessary emergency stop or avoidance maneuver. * Mitigation: Filtering, hysteresis, and consistency checks; classification changes require consecutive confirmations. - Missed Wall Detection: * Effect: Map may remain UNKNOWN or ABSENT while a real wall exists. * Mitigation: Uncertainty model enlargement (±12cm), additional checks combining IR and ultrasonic data; repeated passes through the cell. - Consistency threshold N misconfigured too high: * Effect: Map may fail to update in presence of real change or repeated mis-detections; but static environment assumption reduces risk.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--MAP--1.2.1 LL--MAP--1.2.2 LL--MAP--1.2.3 LL--MAP--1.2.4 LL--MAP--1.3.2 LL--MAP--1.4.3 LL--MAP--1.4.4 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.2 LL--INTERF--1.1.1 LL--INTERF--1.1.2 LL--INTERF--2.1.1 LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.1.1 LL--INTERF--3.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.4.1 LL--HARDWARE--1.1.1 LL--ENV--1.2.1 LL--ENV--1.3.1

Component.Name: Time-Aware Planning Parameters Component
Component.Purpose: Manages configuration parameters related to time-aware planning and speed safety: - Maintains nominal time required to traverse one maze cell at exploration speed. - Provides this parameter to exploration, path planning, and exploration status modules for remaining-time calculations. - Ensures that time-aware planning avoids selecting exploration targets that cannot be reached and mapped within remaining time.

Signal.Name: NominalCellTraversalConfiguration
Signal.Description: See CMP--4.Receive.NominalCellTraversalConfiguration. Contains nominal_cell_traversal_ms and time-aware-planning enable flag.

Signal.Name: TimeLimitParameters
Signal.Description: - global_exploration_time_limit_ms (default 300000). Provided via configuration at init.

Signal.Name: RemainingTimeAndNominalTraversal
Signal.Description: Provided to Frontier Generation: - remaining_time_ms - nominal_cell_traversal_ms.

Signal.Name: TargetSelectionParameters
Signal.Description: Frontier Generation may request or update heuristic parameters: - alpha, beta weights for travel-time vs information gain.

Component.Execution: - At system initialization: * Load nominal_cell_traversal_ms from non-volatile config or compile-time default. * Provide to Exploration Status, Planner, and Frontier Generation components on demand. - No periodic processing besides parameter read queries from other components.
Component.Timing: - Configuration read-only at run-time; used in O(1) queries.
Component.Invariants: - nominal_cell_traversal_ms > 0. - Remains constant throughout mission (no runtime adjustment unless specifically allowed).
Component.Failures: - Misconfigured nominal traversal time: * If too low, targets may be accepted that cannot be reached in time; but global 5-minute time limit still enforced by Exploration Status.

Component.Name: Periodic Control Loop Scheduling Component
Component.Purpose: Provides a deterministic periodic control loop that coordinates: - Sensor sampling via Sensor Abstraction Layer. - Map updates and mapping operations. - Mission Control state machine update. - Path computation updates as required. - Motor command issuing via Motion Control. It exposes a configurable period T, sets sensor sampling rate and control-loop frequency, and provides timing information (timestamps, durations) to other components.

Signal.Name: SensorSamplingTrigger
Signal.Description: On each control-loop iteration: - Trigger to sample ultrasonic and IR sensors once. - Provide sampling period based on configured rate in [10, 50] Hz.

Signal.Name: FilteredSensorData
Signal.Description: Received from Sensor Abstraction; see CMP--10.Transmit.FilteredSensorData.

Signal.Name: MappingUpdateTrigger
Signal.Description: After sensor data acquisition: - Signal to Mapping component to process the new sensor readings and update map.

Signal.Name: MappingCompletionAck
Signal.Description: Optional; indicates mapping operations complete within current cycle.

Signal.Name: MC_Tick
Signal.Description: Once per loop; instructs Mission Control to update FSM based on latest status.

Signal.Name: MC_CommandsOut
Signal.Description: Consolidated MC requests (planning, path execution) that may be processed later within the same loop.

Signal.Name: MotionControlTick
Signal.Description: After MC and Planner updates, trigger Motion Control to issue motor_set commands for this cycle.

Signal.Name: MotorCommands
Signal.Description: Actual motor_set(left_speed,right_speed) to be sent to hardware, limited to at most one call per loop.

Component.Timing: - Control loop period T is configurable so that: * control_loop_frequency = 1/T equals sensor sampling rate between 10 and 50 Hz (default 20 Hz). * Jitter must be within configured tolerance to maintain sensor sampling reliability. - Motor command (motor_set) invoked at most once per iteration at the end of the cycle.
Component.Invariants: - The order of operations within each loop iteration is fixed: sensors -> mapping -> mission control -> planning -> motion. - motor_set is never called more than once per loop. - Control-loop frequency equals sensor sampling frequency.
Component.Failures: - Cycle Overrun: * Condition: Execution of loop body exceeds period T. * Behavior: Next cycle starts immediately; can log overrun or reduce effective sampling rate; reliability requirements may be impacted.

Component.Name: Goal Region Identification and Selection Component
Component.Purpose: After exploration is complete, uses the grid map to: - Identify all contiguous 2x2 blocks of cells all classified FREE. - Determine which of those blocks are reachable from the start cell using the Path Planner. - Select as maze goal region the 2x2 FREE region minimizing path cost from start cell according to the planner cost metric. - Provide selected region to Second-Run planning and Mission Control.

Signal.Name: GridScanForGoalRegions
Signal.Description: Reads all cells to find 2x2 contiguous FREE blocks. For each candidate region: - coordinates: (r,c), (r,c+1), (r+1,c), (r+1,c+1)

Signal.Name: CellStatesForGoalSearch
Signal.Description: Provided by Map: classification of cells used by goal-region identification.

Signal.Name: GoalRegionSelectionQuery
Signal.Description: See CMP--5.Transmit.GoalRegionSelectionQuery for cost-based selection of candidate regions.

Signal.Name: GoalRegionSelectionResult
Signal.Description: See CMP--5.Receive.GoalRegionSelectionResult. Contains selected_region or empty set.

Signal.Name: SelectedGoalRegionToMC
Signal.Description: Data: - selected_goal_region cells (array of up to 4 (row,col) entries). Mission Control passes this to Second Run planning.

Signal.Name: ExplorationCompletedNotification
Signal.Description: From MC or Exploration-Status: indicates exploration completed and triggers goal region determination.

Component.Execution: - On ExplorationCompletedNotification: 1) Enumerate all 2x2 cell blocks in grid: for r in 0..2, c in 0..6: candidate = {(r,c),(r,c+1),(r+1,c),(r+1,c+1)}. if all cells FREE: add to candidates. 2) For each candidate region: - Use Path Planner to determine cost of optimal path from start cell to any of the 4 cells. - If Planner returns NO_PATH, discard candidate (unreachable). 3) Select candidate with minimal cost; mark as selected_goal_region. 4) Provide selected_goal_region to Second Run planning through MC and Path Planner.
Component.Timing: - Invoked once (or rarely) after exploration complete. - Complexity: small due to limited map size; may run in a single control-loop iteration.
Component.Invariants: - selected_goal_region must be a 2x2 block of FREE cells reachable from start. - Only one goal region selected per mission.
Component.Failures: - No candidate 2x2 FREE region: * Behavior: selected_goal_region is empty; Second Run may be skipped or treated as failure. - No candidate reachable from start: * Behavior: treat as above; no second run or auto failure.

Component.Name: Sensor Abstraction and Filtering Component
Component.Purpose: Encapsulates low-level ultrasonic and infrared access functions and provides: - Normalized sensor data structures to higher-level modules. - Sensor sampling at configured rate equal to control loop frequency. - Temporal filtering (moving average or median) to reduce noise. - Handling of invalid/out-of-range sensor readings. - Enforcement of ultrasonic uncertainty model for further processing.

Signal.Name: UltrasonicGetDistance
Signal.Description: Wrapper over ultrasonic_get_distance(); returns raw float distance_cm in robot-forward frame or error code/NaN for invalid.

Signal.Name: IRGetStatus
Signal.Description: Wrappers over infrared_get_dsl_status() and infrared_get_dsr_status(); returns uint8_t 0/1 for each.

Signal.Name: RawHardwareSensorValues
Signal.Description: Raw values returned from hardware APIs used as input into filtering and validation pipeline.

Signal.Name: SensorSamplingTrigger
Signal.Description: See CMP--8.Transmit.SensorSamplingTrigger. Causes one sample of each sensor.

Signal.Name: SamplingRateConfig
Signal.Description: - sampling_rate_hz in [10,50] (default 20). Derived from control loop configuration.

Signal.Name: FilteredSensorData
Signal.Description: See CMP--6.Receive.FilteredSensorData. Provides once per loop: - ultrasonic: filtered distance_cm, timestamp, valid. - IR: left_flag, right_flag, timestamp, valid. Invalid/out-of-range readings replaced with safe defaults or marked invalid.

Signal.Name: SensorDataRequests
Signal.Description: Mapping asks for latest sample; data returned from internal buffers.

Component.Execution: - On each control-loop iteration: 1) Acquire raw ultrasonic distance via ultrasonic_get_distance(). 2) Acquire raw IR statuses via infrared_get_dsl_status() and infrared_get_dsr_status(). 3) Validate data: - For ultrasonic: treat negative, NaN, INF, or known saturation codes as invalid. - For IR: treat values other than defined 0/1 as invalid. 4) Apply temporal filtering: - Maintain sliding window of last K samples (configurable, e.g., 3-5). - For ultrasonic: compute median or moving average of valid samples; if fewer than minimum valid samples, mark measurement invalid or keep last reliable value with separate flag. - For IR: optionally majority vote over last K readings. 5) For ultrasonic, associate each measurement with uncertainty interval ±12cm; store along with filtered distance. 6) Publish filtered sensor data to Mapping and other consumers.
Component.Timing: - Filtering computations are O(K) per cycle with small K. - Sampling performed once per control-loop iteration with jitter < configured tolerance.
Component.Invariants: - Filtered sensor structures always contain valid timestamps from scheduling time base. - Invalid/out-of-range samples never propagate as valid entries.
Component.Failures: - Persistent invalid readings: * Behavior: valid flag remains false; Mapping must treat as unknown environment ahead.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--1.1.1 LL--INTERF--1.1.2 LL--INTERF--2.1.1 LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.2

Component.Name: Pose Estimation Component
Component.Purpose: Estimates robot pose at cell level: - Integrates differential drive kinematics over each control-loop period using motor commands and scheduling information. - Maintains discrete cell index (row, col) and heading aligned with maze axes. - Corrects accumulated drift using map features (walls) and axis alignment. - Detects when robot crosses from one cell center to adjacent cell center and notifies Mapping.

Signal.Name: MotorCommandsForOdometry
Signal.Description: Pose uses latest motor_set commands and control-loop period to integrate kinematics: - left_speed, right_speed in [0,100] - T or measured dt from scheduler.

Signal.Name: MotorSetIssued
Signal.Description: Motion Control informs Pose of motor_set values for current cycle.

Signal.Name: PoseFeatureDetectionQueries
Signal.Description: Pose uses Mapping's wall detections indirectly by reading Mapping outputs or by using input from Mapping about cell boundaries to correct position.

Signal.Name: WallProximityData
Signal.Description: Data from Mapping or sensors about proximity to known walls for pose correction.

Signal.Name: MapFeatureQueries
Signal.Description: Pose looks up cell boundaries and wall positions in map to constrain state corrections.

Signal.Name: MapGeometryParameters
Signal.Description: Cell size (17.2cm), wall thickness (1.6cm), orientation info consistent with environment model.

Signal.Name: CellCenterEntryNotification
Signal.Description: See Mapping component; Pose indicates when cell center is crossed: - from_cell, to_cell, direction.

Signal.Name: PoseUsageInMapping
Signal.Description: Mapping uses get_current_cell and get_heading queries.

Component.Execution: - On each control-loop iteration: 1) Compute longitudinal and rotational displacement from motor speeds and dt using differential-drive kinematics: - v_l, v_r in cm/s from speed commands and calibration. - v = (v_r + v_l)/2; omega = (v_r - v_l)/wheel_base. - Update continuous pose (x,y,theta). 2) Quantize continuous pose to discrete cell (row,col) using grid coordinate system and cell size 17.2cm. 3) Detect if quantized cell differs from previous cell; if so, generate CellCenterEntryNotification. 4) Pose correction: - When Mapping indicates robot is aligned with maze axes and near known walls, adjust theta to nearest axis orientation {0,90,180,270 deg}. - Adjust (x,y) to nearest consistent cell center given wall distances and robot radius 5.5cm. 5) Update heading enumeration accordingly.
Component.Timing: - All operations O(1) per cycle. - Uses dt from Scheduling; dt may vary but is bounded by jitter tolerance.
Component.Invariants: - heading always one of {N,E,S,W} after correction step when alignment condition met. - cell indices remain within 0..3 and 0..7.
Component.Failures: - Kinematic model error: * Over time, drift accumulates; correction with walls mitigates.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--POSE--1.1.1 LL--POSE--1.1.2 LL--POSE--1.1.3 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.1 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.2 LL--SCHED--1.1.1 LL--MAP--1.3.1 LL--MAP--1.2.1 LL--MAP--1.2.2

Component.Name: Motion Control and Motor Interface Component
Component.Purpose: Translates planned cell paths and avoidance requests into motor_set commands: - Implements cell-center motion primitives (move straight one cell, turn in place). - Computes trajectories based on differential drive kinematics and cell/wall geometry. - Applies speed limiting for safety, including maximum translational speed below hardware limit. - Applies rate limiting on speed change per control cycle. - Monitors for emergency stop and collision conditions (from Mapping and Reliability modules). - Executes exploration, return-to-start, and second-run paths with different motion profiles (speeds).

Signal.Name: motor_set
Signal.Description: Hardware API to control differential drive motors: - motor_set(uint8_t left_speed, uint8_t right_speed) where range is [0,100]. Invoked at most once per control loop iteration by this component.

Signal.Name: MotorHardwareStatus
Signal.Description: Optionally, hardware-level status if accessible (e.g., encoder ticks) for improved motion profiling.

Signal.Name: PathExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: See MC component. Contains: - path_id, segment_index - status: IN_PROGRESS, REACHED_GOAL, COLLISION_EMERGENCY_STOP, PATH_INVALIDATED, PLANNING_FAILURE

Signal.Name: MC_PathExecutionCommand
Signal.Description: See MC component. Motion Control begins executing the specified path in the specified mode.

Signal.Name: CurrentMotionProfile
Signal.Description: See Mapping component; used for predicting collisions.

Signal.Name: AvoidanceAndEmergencyCommands
Signal.Description: See Mapping component. Motion Control must honor emergency_stop_request immediately.

Signal.Name: MotionControlTick
Signal.Description: See CMP--8.Transmit.MotionControlTick. Triggers Motion Control operations once per control loop.

Signal.Name: ControlLoopPeriodInfo
Signal.Description: Provides T and dt for converting motor_set steps to physical distances for primitives and speed limits.

Component.Execution: - Motion primitives: * For move forward one cell: - Determine required travel distance: approx 17.2cm from cell center to next. - Given nominal speed v and cycle time T, compute number of cycles N such that N * v * T approximates 17.2cm. - For N cycles, set motor_set(left_speed,right_speed) with equal speeds; after N cycles, stop or proceed to next primitive. * For turning in place: - Compute duration required to rotate 90 degrees based on motor speed difference and wheel base. - For M cycles, set motor_set(l,-l) or comparable values. - Speed limitation: * Use configured max translational speed v_max < 200cm/s, computed using: - sensor sampling rate - emergency_stop threshold - ultrasonic uncertainty model ±12cm. * Guarantee stopping distance smaller than emergency detection distance. - Rate limiting: * Keep previous left/right speeds; enforce |new-left_speed - prev-left_speed| <= delta_left_max, same for right. - Execution per control loop: 1) If emergency_stop_request active: - Immediately issue motor_set(0,0). - Set internal state = EMERGENCY_STOPPED; path execution paused. 2) Else if avoidance maneuver requested: - Override planned path temporarily; execute avoidance primitive sequence. 3) Else: - Follow active path: * Determine next motion primitive step in sequence; send motor_set accordingly. * Monitor Pose and Mapping to check for path completion or invalidation. 4) Report PathExecutionStatus to MC.
Component.Timing: - One motor_set call per control loop at end of cycle. - Computation is O(1) per cycle; primitives may adjust state machines internal to this component.
Component.Invariants: - motor_set is never invoked more than once per control loop cycle. - Commanded speeds always obey: * 0 <= left_speed,right_speed <= 100. * translational_speed_cm_s <= configured v_max for safety. * Rate-of-change per cycle limited by configurable max increment/decrement.
Component.Failures: - Emergency stop due to imminent collision: * Behavior: immediate motor_set(0,0), status COLLISION_EMERGENCY_STOP; path remains unfinished. - Path invalidation: * Condition: Map or Planner identifies segments invalid due to new PRESENT wall or BLOCKED cell. * Behavior: Motion Control stops path execution and signals PATH_INVALIDATED to MC.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--1.2.1 LL--INTERF--1.2.2 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.1 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.2 LL--HARDWARE--2.3.1 LL--HARDWARE--2.3 LL--RUN--1.1.2 LL--RUN--1.2.3 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2

Component.Name: Initialization Module Component
Component.Purpose: Performs system initialization and signals when all hardware and software modules are ready: - Runs hardware init (sensors, motors). - Runs software init (map initialization, configuration load, state resets). - Sets or publishes INIT_COMPLETED for Mission Control.

Signal.Name: MapInitializationTrigger
Signal.Description: Signal to Grid Map to perform Unknown Initialization and Start Cell Initialization.

Signal.Name: MapInitDone
Signal.Description: Ack from Map when initialization complete.

Signal.Name: INIT_COMPLETED_Indication
Signal.Description: After all init tasks done, this flag/event is set/published in main execution context.

Signal.Name: MCAwaitInit
Signal.Description: No explicit signal from MC; MC simply waits for flag.

Signal.Name: ControlLoopStartCommand
Signal.Description: After initialization completed, instruct scheduler to begin periodic control loop.

Signal.Name: SchedulerInit
Signal.Description: Called by init module to set initial T and start control loop.

Component.Execution: - On system boot: 1) Initialize hardware: sensors, motors, timers. 2) Initialize software modules: - Grid Map (CMP--3): Unknown Initialization and Start Cell Setup. - Pose (CMP--11) default to start cell. - Sensor Abstraction (CMP--10) filter buffers. 3) Load configuration: time limits, nominal traversal, speeds, thresholds, N for consistency checks, M margin. 4) Set INIT_COMPLETED flag or publish corresponding event. 5) Start periodic control loop via Scheduler.
Component.Invariants: - INIT_COMPLETED is not set before all dependencies are initialized. - INIT_COMPLETED is set exactly once per system startup.
Component.Failures: - Initialization failure in any component: * Behavior: Do not set INIT_COMPLETED; may signal error externally and avoid starting control loop.

State.Name: IDLE
State.StateAction: - Monitor INIT_COMPLETED indication. - Monitor SensorValidityStatus; ensure at least one valid ultrasonic and IR set. - Do not issue any path or motion commands. - Reset exploration-related timers and flags when mission is reset.

State.Name: EXPLORE
State.StateAction: - Periodically (each control loop) request/update frontier set from Frontier Generation. - Select exploration target using heuristic that considers travel-time estimate and expected information gain while respecting remaining exploration time. - Request path from Planner to selected frontier target if no current valid path. - Command Motion Control to execute exploration path (cell-by-cell). - Monitor Exploration Status events: * EXPLORATION_COMPLETED: set ‘exploration_completed’ flag. * EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT: set ‘exploration_timeout’ flag. - If current exploration path becomes invalid due to new map info, request replanning.

State.Name: RETURN_TO_START
State.StateAction: - Invoke Return-to-Start service: * Compute path from current cell to fixed start cell using Planner. - Command Motion Control to execute return path. - Monitor PathExecutionStatus: * On REACHED_GOAL at start cell: set ‘returned_to_start’ flag. * On NO_PATH or repeated failure: set ‘return_failed’ flag.

State.Name: SECOND_RUN
State.StateAction: - When first entering: * Obtain goal region selection from Goal Region component. * Configure Planner for time-optimal second-run cost model. * Request fastest path from start cell to goal region. - Command Motion Control to execute fastest path with higher speed profile respecting safety constraints. - Monitor path execution: * If robot reaches any FREE goal cell without collision: set second_run_status = SUCCESS. * On emergency stop, path failure, or inability to plan: set second_run_status = FAILURE.

State.Name: COMPLETED
State.StateAction: - Ensure no new motion commands are issued (Motion Control is commanded to stop or maintain safe zero-speed). - Latch final second_run_status for external reporting. - Optionally allow system to reset mission on external command, transitioning back to IDLE.

Transition.Trigger: - INIT_COMPLETED indication becomes true AND - SensorValidityStatus indicates: * has_valid_ultrasonic == true AND * has_valid_ir == true (both left_flag and right_flag present at least once). Evaluated in each control-loop iteration.
Transition.Guard: - Mission not already started (internal flag mission_started == false).
Transition.TransitionAction: - Set mission_started = true. - Notify Exploration Status of entering EXPLORE (start exploration timer). - Initialize exploration-related counters and flags (exploration_completed = false, exploration_timeout = false).

Transition.Trigger: - Receipt of either: * EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event (map completeness), OR * EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event (global 5-min limit). from Exploration-Status component via event queue.
Transition.Guard: - At least one of exploration_completed or exploration_timeout flags is true.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Notify Exploration Status of leaving EXPLORE. - Invoke Return-to-Start service (Planner) with trigger_reason = MAP_COMPLETE or GLOBAL_TIMEOUT. - Set current_mode to RETURN_MODE for Motion Control.

Transition.Trigger: - PathExecutionStatus from Motion Control indicates: * status == REACHED_GOAL AND * current_cell == start_cell. - Exploration has been declared complete (EXPLORATION_COMPLETED previously received, not only TIMEOUT).
Transition.TransitionAction: - Set returned_to_start = true. - Notify Second-Run initialization that start cell reached. - Request Goal Region selection and configure Planner for fastest path.

Transition.Trigger: - Return path planning or execution fails: * Planner responds NO_PATH for RETURN_TO_START, OR * Motion Control reports repeated COLLISION_EMERGENCY_STOP or PATH_INVALIDATED leading to abort.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Command Motion Control to stop (motor_set(0,0)). - Set second_run_status = FAILURE.

Transition.Trigger: - Second run completes or aborts: * PathExecutionStatus indicates REACHED_GOAL within goal region (success), OR * PathExecutionStatus indicates COLLISION_EMERGENCY_STOP, PATH_INVALIDATED with no successful replanning, or planning failure (failure).
Transition.TransitionAction: - Command Motion Control to stop (motor_set(0,0)). - Latch second_run_status in MC for external reporting.

Transition.Trigger: - Severe unrecoverable failure detected during exploration (e.g., repeated emergency stops with no possible path, severe sensor failure).
Transition.TransitionAction: - Command Motion Control to stop (motor_set(0,0)). - Set second_run_status = FAILURE.

Transition.Trigger: - External reset command or system restart.
Transition.Guard: - Allowed only after mission fully stopped and external command available.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Reset all internal MC flags, clear event queues as appropriate, re-enter initialization flow.

Data.Content: Coordinate System Definition: - Maze grid has 4 rows (0..3) and 8 columns (0..7). - Row index increases from start row toward far end; column index increases from left to right when viewed from start. - Start cell is fixed at (row=0, col=0). - Heading enumeration: * NORTH: -row direction * EAST: +col direction * SOUTH: +row direction * WEST: -col direction. - Wall indexing: * Horizontal walls: indices (r,c,H) between (r,c) and (r+1,c). * Vertical walls: indices (r,c,V) between (r,c) and (r,c+1).

Ultrasonic Uncertainty and Clearance Configuration: - Ultrasonic uncertainty interval: ±12 cm, applied to measured distance d as [d-12, d+12]. - Robot modeled as circle radius R = 5.5 cm. - Minimum clearance margin M: configurable value M > 0 cm (e.g., default 2-4 cm). - Emergency stop detection: * emergency_threshold_distance = f(current_speed, sensor_sampling_rate, uncertainty_interval). * Condition for emergency stop: if (d - 12cm) <= stopping_distance + M then immediate stop. - Map-based clearance checks use: * wall positions at cell boundaries (distance approx (cell_size/2) - wall_thickness/2 - R).
Data.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 LL--HARDWARE--1.1.1 LL--ENV--1.2.1 LL--ENV--1.3.1

Data.Content: Sensor Filtering Parameters and Algorithms: - Sampling rate: configurable samplerate_hz in [10,50], default 20 Hz; T = 1000 / samplerate_hz ms. - Ultrasonic filter: * Window size K_ultra (default 3). * Use median filter over last K_ultra valid readings. - IR filter: * Window size K_ir (default 3). * Use majority vote over last K_ir readings for each sensor (left/right). - Consistency check parameter N: * N in [2,10], default 3. * For cell/wall classification reversal attempts, require N consecutive contradictory readings before update.

Exploration Target Selection Heuristic Details: - Travel-time component: * Use Path Planner's cost model; convert path cost to estimated time: estimated_time_ms = cost_units * nominal_cell_traversal_ms / base_cost_unit. - Information gain estimation: * For candidate frontier cell f: - Look at its 4-neighborhood; count UNKNOWN cells and walls within 1-cell radius. - expected_new_cells = count of UNKNOWN neighbors not yet visible from other frontiers. - Combined heuristic: * H(f) = alpha * estimated_time_ms - beta * expected_new_cells. * alpha > 0, beta >= 0 configurable. - Time constraint: * Only consider f if estimated_time_ms <= remaining_time_ms.

Motion Profile Parameters: - Exploration mode: * target_speed_explore_cm_s: lower speed for safe mapping. - Return-to-start mode: * target_speed_return_cm_s: moderate speed, similar to exploration but can be slightly higher. - Second-run mode: * target_speed_second_run_cm_s: highest auto speed, still < v_max computed for safety. - v_max calculation: * v_max < 200 cm/s hardware limit. * Choose v_max such that: stopping_distance(v_max) + uncertainty_margin + M < min(expected_wall_distance) over planning horizon.
